channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Marathon Devs Announce Recon Changes Ahead of Next Patch
item.content:encoded:
The developers of Marathon have a major update scheduled for April 14. However, they have some notable changes they want to share with players early so players get a sense of what’s coming.

The changes shared are related specifically to the Recon Shell. Ahead of the full release of passion notes for version 1.0.6, the Bungie developers have shared changes coming to Echo Pulse and Tracker Drone.

Marathon Recon Updates Coming on April 14

In a Twitter post, the Marathon Dev Team shared that the echo pulse ability will have its visibility to enemies reduced. This should make it harder for enemies to gauge whether they’re within range of an attack. Also, if you’re a Runner who has used a Signal Jammer before the Echo Pulse, you’ll appear as UESC.

For the Tracker Drone, they promised that this ability will get more aggressive. In the next patch, they are increasing the drones’ tracking strength and allowing them to find a new target if they can’t reach the initial one.

The developers have confirmed that they plan on supporting the game for many years. Players can read their comments here. For more on Marathon, check out our full review of the game at this link.

Also, you can check out the notes from the previous patch at this link.

Have you begun playing Marathon yet? If so, what are your thoughts on the game? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Pokémon Pokopia-Inspired Experience Opens in London
item.content:encoded:
A Pokémon Pokopia-inspired experience has opened in central London.

Pokémon Pokopia is a game exclusive to the Nintendo Switch 2 and has quickly taken over many players’ lives. A game that takes elements from Dragon Quest Builders, Minecraft, and Animal Crossing, the game has quickly become the best-selling Pokémon spin-off game of all time.

Pokémon Pokopia Immersive Space Opens at ‘The Mount’

Pokopia Garden is now open in Acton, London. Opening on March 31, the experience is free to the public and will be open for the next 6 months until September 30. An Instagram post notes that it was created in collaboration with local businesses and features a curated section of plants alongside Pokémon-inspired terrariums.

“Open Pokémon GO to find a special PokéStop and Gym too,” the post encourages players. It’s also noted that Pokopia plushes will be available at the Pokémon Center in the UK this summer.

Pokémon Pokopia Proves to Be A Killer App for the Nintendo Switch 2

Pokémon Pokopia has not only become a standout seller in the world of Pokémon, but it’s also become a premier reason for players to pick up the Nintendo Switch successor.

As players continue to wait for a new 3D Mario experience and the first Legend of Zelda experience on the Switch 2, Pokopia stands beside Donkey Kong Bananza as a top-tier exclusive game for the new Switch.

Learn more about the future of the Nintendo console and the potential return of Star Fox here.

Have you started playing Pokopia? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Free Amazon Luna Games For April 2026 Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Check out the slew of free games that will be available for free via the Amazon Luna service.

Unlike other gaming launchers, Luna also allows players to connect their GOG and Epic Games Store accounts. Free games for the month include several titles from those services, including XCOM: Enemy Unknown from GOG and Total War: Pharaoh Dynasties from Epic.

Other free games to claim throughout the month include Monster Harvest from the Epic Games Store and Pinball Spire from GOG. The full list of free games will be below.

Amazon Luna’s Claim Games for April 2026

April 2

  • XCOM: Enemy Unknown Complete Pack
  • Total War: Pharaoh Dynasties

April 9

  • A Rat’s Quest: The Way Back Home
  • King of Retail

April 16

  • Snake Core
  • Monster Harvest
  • Detective Agency: Gray Tie Collector’s Edition

April 23

  • Neo Cab
  • The Pale Beyond

April 30

  • KinnikuNeko: Super Muscle Cat
  • Fantasy General
  • Pinball Spire

Other free games available right now include Tiny Tina’s Wonderland and Sid Meier’s Civilization 6. Amazon Prime subscribers get access to standard Amazon Luna subscriptions, and premium ones start at $9.99 per month.

Games such as Dave the Diver and Indiana Jones and the Great Circle are also available on the service. Even Sega’s greatest mascot, Sonic the Hedgehog, has multiple games on the platform for premium subscribers.

For more free games from the month of April, check out what’s coming on PlayStation Plus.

How do you feel about these free games? Do you subscribe to Amazon Luna? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

PlayStation Games on PC Took 3 Years to Earn $300M

The LinkedIn account of a former PlayStation employee offers new insights into how PlayStation generated profits by bringing its games to PC.

Sony Announces Acquisition of Cinemersive Labs

Sony will be acquiring Cinemersive Labs and moving them to their Visual Computing Group. Here are more details.

Raven Software Co-Founder Brian Raffel Announces Retirement

Raven Software’s co-founder has announced his retirement after spending 36 years at the helm. Here are more details.

Xbox FanFest Tour is Coming to 8 Cities in 2026

Xbox has announced that the FanFest Tour for the 25th Anniversary will be arriving to 8 cities. Here are more details.

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1027)—Answers for Friday, April 3
item.content:encoded:
It’s the end of another week, but hopefully that NYT Connections streak is still going strong! As you coast into the weekend (which is Easter, if you happen to celebrate), go safe in the knowledge that you’ve got the best NYT Connections hints and answers, right here. In the guide below, I’ve outlined everything you need to know to beat today’s puzzle.

Just read on to get today’s NYT Connections hints and solutions!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (April 3)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • PETTY
  • CRUISE
  • LONG
  • ZOMBIE
  • HURRICANE
  • JONES
  • GROUND
  • COLLINS
  • MISSION
  • SMALL
  • LUST
  • SNIDE
  • MEAN
  • THIRST
  • DAMAGE
  • ROCKS

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Words you might associate with childish behavior
  • Green – Words to describe wanting something real bad
  • Blue – Different types of cocktail glasses
  • Purple – Words that can go before ‘control’

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, April 3:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, April 3 (#1027)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – MEAN, PETTY, SMALL, SNIDE
  • Green – JONES, LONG, LUST, THIRST
  • Blue – COLLINS, HURRICANE, ROCKS, ZOMBIE
  • Purple – CRUISE, DAMAGE, GROUND, MISSION

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-connections-hints-solutions-april-3-1027/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PlayStation Games on PC Took 3 Years to Earn $300M
item.content:encoded:
The LinkedIn account of a former PlayStation employee offers new insights into how PlayStation generated profits by bringing its games to PC.

Jerry Liu was previously the PC Planner and Insights Manager at PlayStation from January 2021 to June 2023. On his LinkedIn page, he notes that he increased the division’s net revenue from $0 to $300 million during his time overseeing the brand.

Some of his responsibilities included providing insights to persuade leadership to adopt a more aggressive pricing strategy, which helped increase the business unit’s gross revenue by 25%. Additionally, he analyzed the PlayStation catalog to identify valuable underutilized games to bring to the PC market.

In contrast, PS4 and PS5 software sales hit that milestone in a single year.

PlayStation Reportedly Moving Away from PC

Recently, reports suggested that PlayStation may be stepping back from porting its single-player games to PC. In March, it was reported that plans to release Ghost of Yotei and other titles on Steam were canceled.

Over the past six years, titles like Returnal, God of War, God of War Ragnarok, the Spider-Man games, and the Horizon series have seen success after being ported to PC. However, research shows that PlayStation ports on PC tend to have a low player share. Find out more about that here.

What do you think of this news? How do you feel about the move away from PC by PlayStation? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: League of Legends Receives Brilliant Quality of Life Update
item.content:encoded:
League of Legends has added another huge quality-of-life update, which could be a massive upgrade for many players.

Riot Games has been on a mission to make the MOBA more accessible and new player-friendly ahead of the huge ‘League Next’ update, which will overhaul the entire game next year.

The latest change is a new setting released with LoL Patch 26.7 titled “Learn Spell On Cast.”

When enabled, players can hit the key for a move their character hasn’t learned in-game yet to immediately learn it.

Usually, players would have to move their mouse to the bottom of the screen and click on the arrow of the move they want to learn or hold down Ctrl and press the key to level it.

The latter is seen as the most effective as it means you remain in full control of your character and can quickly learn or upgrade a move during a hectic team fight.

Now, Riot has made it easier for newer as less mechanically blessed players, as this new setting means players will no longer awkwardly have to hold down Ctrl.

This brilliant new feature is very beneficial as it rewards players for not immediately levelling new skills.

Instead, during the laning phase, players can wait to see if they need an extra damage spell or an escape tool once they hit level two in lane.

Then they can instinctively react to the scenario by casting the ability they want. Just note, the unlearned ability won’t cast on the first press.

Another thing to keep in mind with this feature is that it can also be used to put points into upgrading already learned abilities.

This means if you’re spamming abilities during a fight and level up, you may accidentally put more points into an ability than you wanted.

This is something to be cautious of during the start of ARAM matches, where you start with three skill points.

Will you be turning on this new setting? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And in a more controversial change, Riot Games has confirmed voice chat is coming to League of Legends.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/league-of-legends-receives-brilliant-quality-of-life-update/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: April Fools’ Joke Confirms Locke as Next League of Legends Champion
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has finally confirmed that the next League of Legends champion will be Demacian comic character Locke — accidentally revealing this through an April Fools’ joke.

Riot’s LoL game director Pu ‘Pupulasers’ Liu confirmed that all the rumours about Locke being the game’s 173rd champion are true.

In a now-deleted tweet, Pupulasers joked:

“Some players believe that League of Legends has too many champions. Turns out they’re absolutely right, and 172 is the maximum number of champions we can have in League. When Locke releases, one champion will be deleted from League forever.”

This is the first time that Riot has directly mentioned Locke as a champion. He was introduced as a Demacian character through the “For the Fallen” motion comic released by the publisher earlier this year.

This sticks with the Demacian theme for the season, and follows on from the release of the Shyvana VGU rework.

Not too much is known about Locke so far.

There have also been plenty of rumours, including one that Locke will be an AP jungler or mid laner who uses giant nails as part of his kit, which supposedly leaked images seemingly confirm.

In the aforementioned comic, a hooded figure, believed to be Locke, speaks with a bartender who warns him that “no one that goes into those woods comes back, at least not the same as they were.”

This could relate to the dark shadows that Sona fought off in a forest during this year’s cinematic and would tie in nicely with the leaked theme for LoL’s next season.

Other than that, a fact we know about Locke is that he will be the only new LoL champion released in 2026.

And we also know it’ll probably be a bit longer until the new champion releases, with Locke expected around halfway through the year.

This information comes from League Studio head Andrei ‘Meddler’ van Roon, who said as much in a Reddit comment earlier this year:

“One new champ this year, around the middle of the year. Most of the team that normally make new champs are helping out with the large update that we’ll talk about post-MSI or so.

“2027 will depend a lot on how progress on said large update goes and how players respond to the different parts of it.”

This could also be the mystery project Phreak stepped away from LoL’s live design to work on. Many are presuming that this could be the previously announced League Next update, which will upgrade the game’s client and redesign the Summoner’s Rift map amongst other major changes.

Would you rather Riot focus on new champions or more reworks and VGUs? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And in another upcoming update, the LoL Worlds 2025 champions skins may now be confirmed.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android
